steps.title: Locate the list boundary
steps.content: The sentence lists three behavioral economists: Ai Hisano, George Akerlof, and Iris Bohnet. Each person's name is paired with identifying information, and that identifying information contains commas, such as a senior lecturer at Kyoto University. Because the list items already contain internal commas, the boundary between major list items needs stronger punctuation.

steps.title: Separate complex items
steps.content: Choice A, University, George Akerlof,, relies only on commas, making it hard to tell where Hisano's information ends and Akerlof's begins. Choice B, University George Akerlof,, gives no punctuation between the two people. Choice D, University; George Akerlof;, uses a semicolon after Akerlof and detaches his identifying phrase, a professor at Georgetown University.

steps.title: Preserve Akerlof's description
steps.content: Choice C, University; George Akerlof,, uses a semicolon to separate Hisano's full list item from Akerlof's. The comma after George Akerlof then correctly attaches his appositive before the next semicolon introduces Iris Bohnet. The answer is C.
